title: Sepsis review documentation

examination.text: Supports temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, blood pressure, consciousness, oxygen saturation, skin signs, urine output, fever or rigors, and examination for the suspected infection source.
examination.rationale: Supports entered physiological observations and source examination for ICU sepsis review; no clinical action is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, blood pressure, consciousness, oxygen saturation, skin signs, urine output, fever or rigors, and examination for the suspected infection source.

source.title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over: recognition, assessment and early management

exact_section.heading: Recommendation 1.3 — history, observations, circulation, respiration, skin, urine output, and source examination

investigations.text: Supports clinician-selected blood and microbiology testing, urinalysis, chest radiography, and abdominal or pelvic imaging to identify an infection source.
investigations.rationale: Supports clinician-selected source investigations for ICU sepsis review; no clinical action is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-selected blood and microbiology testing, urinalysis, chest radiography, and abdominal or pelvic imaging to identify an infection source.

source.title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over: recognition, assessment and early management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.8 and 1.11 — source identification and investigations

follow_up.text: Supports suspected-infection and fever presentation documentation, source symptoms, risk factors, and recognition that sepsis can present with nonspecific illness.
follow_up.rationale: Supports suspected infection, source and risk-factor documentation for ICU sepsis review; no clinical action is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports suspected-infection and fever presentation documentation, source symptoms, risk factors, and recognition that sepsis can present with nonspecific illness.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Source, cultures, observations, organ dysfunction, lactate, antimicrobials, fluids, supports and response are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, escalation or disposition is generated for ICU sepsis review.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
